title: lespedeza bicolor
intro: Lespedeza bicolor, also known as the purple bush clover, is a perennial flowering plant native to Asia and North America. It is a legume that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an anti-cancer agent. The herbal preparations of Lespedeza bicolor include dried flowers, leaves, and stems that can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
